2;../dataset/rule_figures/Figure89.jpg;HBN 00-02
column2: Figure 89;"Side elevation: Vertical grabrail should be 600 mm. There should be 1100 mm to mid-point of vertical grabrail. There should be also another grabrail which should be horizontal or at 15 degrees to the surfaceof the toilet pan seat cover. Above this grabrail there should be a toilet roll holder with 800-1000 mm from floor level. There should be 680 mm from floor surface until axis of horisontal grabnail or until middle point of lower fastening of grabnail.

column1: Front elevation: The height of a toilet seat for adults should be 475-480 mm
column2: and for children - 420 mm. There should be 680 mm from floor surface until axis of horisontal grabnail.

column1: Top view: The drawing illustrates the spatial layout around a semi-ambulant toilet
column2: showing a required usage zone of 800 mm wide by 600 mm deep in front of the pan. Space to use at low level should be 300 mm from the axis of a pan to the both sides (600 mm wide in sum). A minimum distance to the fastening of the horizontal grabrail should be at 300 mm from the toilet centerline. A horizontal grabrail must be mounted 80 mm from the adjacent wall. Disposal bin zone is marked adjacent to the WC on the right. The toilet pan must be between 55 and 520 mm deep. This view also highlights ""space for pan"" which must be 600mm deep. ";;

column1: 4;../dataset/rule_figures/Figure67.jpg;HBN 00-02
column2: Figure 67;"Figure 67: Space requirements for trolley access shower

column1: The drawing presents a plan view of a trolley access shower layout
column2: showing the required spatial dimensions for staff and equipment movement. In the center of the room is a shower trolley
column3: with a staff member positioned on the right side holding a shower head connected by a hose to the shower equipment on the right.

column1: The total width of the shower area is 2360 mm
column2: divided into three zones: 800 mm on each side marked as ‘Minimum space for staff to wash the patient’ and 760 mm in the middle for the trolley. The overall length required for a 1900 mm long trolley is 2700 mm
column3: including a 600 mm minimum space beyond the end of the trolley to allow staff to pass and manoeuvre.

column1: At the upper edge of the plan
column2: a toilet pan or sluice is shown
column3: with a floor gully positioned near the right wall. Between the gully and coworker
column4: the plan indicates a staff emergency ceiling pull cord (E). The distance between the floor gully axis and the wall at the trolley head is indicated as 200 mm. The coloured (purple-pink) area represents the required activity zone
column5: including the minimum space for staff to pass and perform patient care activities.";;

column1: 5;../dataset/rule_figures/Figure53.jpg;HBN 00-02
column2: Figure 53;"Figure 53: Space requirements for shower with adjustable tip-up shower seat

column1: The drawing illustrates the spatial requirements for a shower room equipped with an adjustable tip-up shower seat
column2: showing both a side elevation (top) and a plan view (bottom)
column3: together with dimensional constraints and activity zones for independent use

column1: The upper diagram (side elevation) shows the shower enclosure in section.
A fixed shower head is located 500 mm from the right-hand wall. The zone for shower controls is indicated centrally on the wall
column2: aligned with the seated user position. On the right is the range of the adjustable and removable shower head with vertical dimensions of 1000 mm
column3: marked at a height of 900 mm to 1900 mm above the floor.

column1: The depth of the shower seat required for Approved Document M / BS 8300 is marked as 650 mm. The adjustable tip-up seat can have a range of 200 mm
column2: starting at a height of 480 mm from the floor to 680 mm.

column1: The lower diagram (plan view) presents the layout from above
column2: showing the adjustable tip-up seat mounted on the right-hand wall and the surrounding manoeuvring space.

column1: The seat is positioned so that its centreline is approximately 780 mm from the wall to the top of the view
column2: (the wall with an annotation indicating ""assisted wheelchair transfer restricted from this side""). The shower curtain should be 1200 mm deep and 920 mm wide in front of the seat. A diagonal dimension labelled “600 mm max reach from seat in corner position for independent use” indicates the user’s reach envelope when seated.

column1: The grabrail should be 320 mm from the center of the seat to the axis of the grabrail.

The shaded polygonal purple zone represents required spaces for assisted wheelchair transfer. This zone provides for a distance of approximately 1190 mm in front of the curtain and a distance of approximately 940 mm from the centre line of the seat along the wall to which the seat is mounted. The widest distance from the centre line of the seat to the edge of the polygonal zone is approximately 940 + 280 = 1220 mm.

Space for mobile hoist transfer (front access) occupies the left part of the shaded area and takes around 1400 mm (min 1150 mm) from the centerline of seat. It is around 2400 to 2200 mm wide to the front of the wall to which seat is mounted. Space for mobile hoist transfer (side access) is located directly below the seat
column2: forming an rectangular area with a depth of 2400 (min 2200) mm from the centerline of the seat and width of 1400 mm (min 1150 mm).

column1: The total required area of the room is around 2400 mm +780 mm =3180 mm deep. The width should be around 2400 (min 2200).";;
